label: Compiler Design
description: The course topic.

label: Compiler Phases
description: The different stages of the compiler.

label: Language Translator
description: Needed to convert human readable source code into machine code.

label: Human Readable Source Code
description: The input to a language translator.

label: Machine Code
description: The output of a language translator.

label: Pre-processor
description: A phase of the language translator that converts high-level language code into pure high-level language code.

label: High-Level Language Code
description: The input to the pre-processor.

label: Pure High-Level Language Code
description: The output of the pre-processor, where required header files are embedded and pre-processor directives are omitted; input to the compiler.

label: Compiler (Specific)
description: A phase of the language translator that converts pure high-level language code into equivalent assembly language code.

label: Assembler
description: A phase of the language translator, following the compiler.

label: Linker and Loader
description: The final phase of the language translator.

label: Assembly Language Code
description: The equivalent code produced by the compiler from pure high-level language code.

label: Lexical Analysis Phase
description: The first of the six phases of the compiler where the lexical analyzer processes input.

label: Lexical Analyzer
description: The component that takes care of the lexical analysis process, taking lexemes as inputs and generating tokens.

label: Lexemes
description: Inputs to the lexical analyzer, similar to words, where groups in their entirety convey meaning.

label: Tokens
description: The output of the lexical analyzer, representing the meanings of the lexemes.

label: Regular Expressions (Regexs)
description: Used by the lexical analyzer to recognize tokens.

label: Finite Automata
description: An equivalent illustration used to demonstrate regular expressions for identifiers.

label: Type 3 / Regular Grammars
description: Grammars used by the lexical analyzer for examining lexemes, belonging to the family of grammars for formal languages.

label: Stream of Tokens
description: The output of the lexical analysis phase, serving as input to the syntax analysis phase.

label: Syntax Analysis Phase
description: The second phase of the compiler where the syntax analyzer processes a stream of tokens.

label: Syntax Analyzer / Parser
description: The component in control of the syntax analysis phase, analyzing the stream of tokens following specific production rules.

label: Type 2 / Context-Free Grammars (CFG)
description: Grammars that the syntax analyzer depends on.

label: Production Rules
description: Specific set of rules used by the parser to form the parse tree.

label: Parse Tree
description: The output of the syntax analysis phase, illustrating the grammatical structure.

label: Start Symbol (S)
description: A non-terminal symbol in production rules that can be rewritten to derive expressions.

label: Terminals
description: The set of elements in a grammar like identifiers, operators, and semicolons.

label: Variables / Non-terminals
description: The set of elements in a grammar like S, E, T, F, which can be rewritten.

label: Yield of the Parse Tree
description: Found by traversing the parse tree top to bottom, left to right, taking notes of only the terminals.

label: Syntax Error
description: An error detected if the yield of the parse tree and the provided stream of tokens are not the same.

label: Semantic Analysis Phase
description: The third phase of the compiler where the semantic analyzer performs logical analysis.

label: Semantic Analyzer
description: The component that takes the parse tree and produces a semantically verified parse tree.

label: Semantically Verified Parse Tree
description: The output of the semantic analysis phase, indicating logical correctness.

label: Type Checking
description: A responsibility of the semantic analyzer.

label: Area Bound Checking
description: A responsibility of the semantic analyzer.

label: Correctness of Scope Resolution
description: A responsibility of the semantic analyzer.

label: Logical Analysis
description: The process performed by the semantic analyzer on the parse tree to check for meaningfulness.

label: Intermediate Code Generation Phase
description: The fourth phase of the compiler where intermediate code is produced.

label: Intermediate Code Generator
description: The component that takes a semantically verified parse tree and produces intermediate code.

label: Intermediate Code
description: The output of the intermediate code generator, serving as a machine-independent representation of the code.

label: Three-Address Code (TAC)
description: A popular intermediate code representation where statements have at most three addresses.

label: Temporary Variable
description: Variables (e.g., T0, T1) created by the intermediate code generator to store intermediate results.

label: Address
description: Refers to the addresses of variables within three-address code statements.

label: Frontend
description: The collective term for the first four phases of the compiler (Lexical Analysis, Syntax Analysis, Semantic Analysis, Intermediate Code Generation).

label: Code Optimization Phase
description: The fifth phase of the compiler where intermediate code is optimized.

label: Code Optimizer
description: The component that takes intermediate code and generates optimized code.

label: Optimized Code
description: The output of the code optimizer, a reduced or improved version of the intermediate code.

label: Machine Dependent Optimization
description: A type of code optimization that depends on the target machine architecture.

label: Machine Independent Optimization
description: A type of code optimization that is not specific to any particular machine architecture.

label: Backend
description: The collective term for the last two phases of the compiler (Code Optimization and Target Code Generation).

label: Target Code Generation Phase
description: The sixth and final phase of the compiler, where the final assembly code is produced.

label: Target Code Generator
description: The component that takes optimized code and finally produces the assembly code segment.

label: Assembly Code Segment
description: The final machine-specific output of the target code generator.

label: Mnemonic
description: Instructions used in assembly code (e.g., MOV, IMUL).

label: Registers (EAX, EDX)
description: CPU components like EAX and EDX used to store data during assembly code execution.

label: D Word PTR
description: A data word pointer used in assembly code.

label: RBP (Register-Based Pointer)
description: A register-based pointer used in assembly code.

label: LEX (Tool)
description: A standard lexical analyzer generator used for implementing the lexical analysis phase.

label: YACC (Yet Another Compiler Compiler) (Tool)
description: An LALR parser generator used for implementing the syntax analysis phase.

label: LALR Parser Generator
description: A type of parser generator that YACC is.

label: LANS C Compiler (Tool)
description: A software platform used to implement the entire frontend of a C language compiler for an embedded processor.
